commits.message: fix(github): detect GHE instances for URL validation
commits.author: Akshay Pant

release_note.text:
Reject Repository CRs with malformed GitHub URLs that include extra
path segments (e.g., https://github.com/org/repo/extra). These URLs
previously passed admission and were truncated during token scoping,
allowing bypass of namespace guards.
Add GitHub Enterprise detection to accurately validate repository
URLs. Detects GHE via Server header and /api/v3/meta endpoint,
then enforces org/repo format without additional path segments.
This prevents malformed URLs during admission and token scoping.

release_note.text: With this update, Pipelines-as-Code (PaC) introduces a configurable prefix for GitOps commands to prevent execution conflicts when multiple CI systems, like Prow, are active on the same repository. Users can now define a custom string that must precede standard commands, such as /pac-retest instead of /retest, ensuring the controller only triggers for its designated instructions. This feature effectively eliminates redundant builds and command collisions, providing a seamless experience for teams running hybrid CI environments.

commits.message: fix(opscomments): skip key=value args as PR names
commits.author: Akshay Pant

release_note.text:
/test with custom parameters now correctly triggers on-comment PipelineRuns
Previously, when a PipelineRun used an on-comment annotation like /test and a user posted a comment such as /test 
custom1=value to pass custom parameters, the key=value argument was mistakenly treated as a PipelineRun name. This caused
the comment to skip on-comment annotation matching entirely, so the PipelineRun never triggered. Now, key=value arguments
are properly ignored during name matching, allowing /test with custom parameters to correctly match and trigger the intended
PipelineRun.

commits.message: feat(gitea): implement GetTaskURI for remote task resolution
commits.author: Akshay Pant

release_note.text:
Remote Task Resolution for Gitea/Forgejo
Pipelines-as-Code now supports resolving remote taskRef URLs when using Gitea or Forgejo as your Git provider.
Previously, referencing a Tekton Task hosted in a remote Gitea/Forgejo repository via URL was not supported — the provider would silently ignore the reference. With this change, you can now point to Tasks stored in any Gitea/Forgejo repository using standard web URLs, and Pipelines-as-Code will fetch them using authenticated API calls.
Supported URL formats:Branch: https://gitea.example.com/org/repo/src/branch/main/path/to/task.yamlTag: https://gitea.example.com/org/repo/src/tag/v1.0/path/to/task.yamlCommit SHA: https://gitea.example.com/org/repo/src/commit/abc123/path/to/task.yaml
Both /src/ and /raw/ URL styles are accepted.
Example PipelineRun annotation:
```
pipelinesascode.tekton.dev/task: "https://gitea.example.com/my-org/my-tasks/src/branch/main/tasks/my-task.yaml"
```

commits.message: fix(github): scope App token to triggering repo
commits.author: Akshay Pant

release_note.text:
Security: GitHub App Token Scoping
GitHub App installation tokens are now scoped to only the triggering repository by default. Previously, when a PipelineRun
referenced remote tasks or pipelines via annotations, the token could access any private repository under the same GitHub
App installation. Now, unless additional repositories are explicitly configured via secret-github-app-scope-extra-repos in
the pipelines-as-code ConfigMap, the token is restricted to the repository that triggered the event.
This also applies to incoming webhooks – the token is scoped using the repository name from the Repository CR URL.
Action required: If your PipelineRuns fetch remote tasks or pipelines from other private repositories in the same
installation, add those repositories to secret-github-app-scope-extra-repos in your pipelines-as-code ConfigMap
(comma-separated owner/repo format).
Bug Fix: Remote Pipeline/Task Cache Mutation
Fixed a bug where cached remote Pipeline and Task objects could be mutated during inlining, causing subsequent PipelineRuns
referencing the same remote resource to receive corrupted definitions. Cached objects are now deep-copied before use.

release_note.text:
Add support for centralized TLS configuration on OpenShift. When
`enableCentralTLSConfig` is set to `true` in `TektonConfig.spec.platforms.openshift`,
Tekton components automatically inherit TLS settings (minimum version, cipher suites)
from the cluster's APIServer TLS security profile. Changes to the APIServer profile
are detected and propagated to components without manual intervention. TektonResult
is the first component to support this; other components will follow.

release_note.text:
Migrated operator metrics from OpenCensus to OpenTelemetry.

ACTION REQUIRED:

1. Configuration key change
   Replace metrics.backend-destination with metrics-protocol in your tekton-config-observability ConfigMap.

2. Infrastructure metric renaming

   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ Old Metric Name (OpenCensus)                                     │ New Metric Name (OpenTelemetry)                    │
   ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_workqueue_adds_total                   │ kn_workqueue_adds_total                            │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_workqueue_depth                        │ kn_workqueue_depth                                 │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_workqueue_queue_latency_seconds        │ kn_workqueue_queue_duration_seconds                │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_workqueue_work_duration_seconds        │ kn_workqueue_process_duration_seconds              │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_workqueue_unfinished_work_seconds      │ kn_workqueue_unfinished_work_seconds               │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_client_latency                         │ http_client_request_duration_seconds               │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_client_results                         │ kn_k8s_client_http_response_status_code_total      │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_go_*                                   │ go_*                                               │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_reconcile_count                        │ removed (use kn_workqueue_adds_total)              │
   │ tekton_operator_lifecycle_reconcile_latency                      │ removed (use kn_workqueue_process_duration_seconds)│
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

3. The following operator specific metrics are removed in this version since they were unused and not serving the purpose as expected. New metrics would be added as required in future work:

tekton_operator_pipeline_reconcile_count	
tekton_operator_trigger_reconcile_count
tekton_operator_chains_reconciled
tekton_operator_results_reconciled

commits.message: Add centrally managed TLS configuration for console-plugin nginx
commits.author: Jawed khelil

release_note.text:
The console-plugin nginx server now inherits TLS settings from the centrally managed APIServer TLS Profile, improving Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) readiness compliance on OpenShift.
The nginx server reads TLS configuration from api server profile (TLS_MIN_VERSION, TLS_CIPHER_SUITES, TLS_CURVE_PREFERENCES) and injects them into nginx.conf at runtime.
When no tls paramters are set, the server falls back to secure defaults (TLSv1.2 and TLSv1.3).
Cipher suites are intentionally delegated to nginx's built-in secure defaults.

release_note.text:
Bug fix: OpenShift Pipelines operator now uninstalls cleanly via the Console UI.
Previously, operator admission webhooks (namespace.operator.tekton.dev,
proxy.operator.tekton.dev) could still be registered while their backend service
was already removed during uninstall, causing TektonConfig finalization to fail
and leaving the Subscription, CSV, and operator deployment behind in
openshift-operators. The webhooks are now explicitly removed at the start of
finalization before namespace label cleanup runs.

commits.author: Anitha Natarajan

release_note.text: Chains metrics implementation migrates from the deprecated OpenCensus library to OpenTelemetry. The observability config key in tekton-chains-config-observability is renamed from metrics.backend-destination to metrics-protocol. Knative infrastructure metric names are updated. Chains-specific signing metrics retain their existing names. Metrics can now be pushed directly to any OTLP-compatible backend via otlp/grpc or otlp/http protocols.

commits.message: Add TLS security profile support for core interceptors
commits.author: Jawed khelil

release_note.text: Core interceptors now honor the TLS security profile injected by the Tekton operator via TLS_MIN_VERSION, TLS_CIPHER_SUITES, and TLS_CURVE_PREFERENCES environment variables, allowing cluster-wide TLS policy enforcement on OpenShift.
